metric_name: SAP SD benchmark — HP NetServer LX Pro / Oracle7 (May 1996)
metric_value: 900 users; 4,544 SAPS; 272,667 dialogue steps/hour; WinNT 3.51; 2,048 MB RAM

metric_name: SAP SD benchmark — HP NetServer LX Pro / SQL Server 6.5 (August 1996)
metric_value: 774 users; 3,900 SAPS; 234,000 dialogue steps/hour; WinNT 4.0; 2,048 MB RAM
notes: SQL Server 6.5 vs Oracle7 = ~17% fewer users and ~15% lower SAPS

metric_name: SAP SD benchmark — Data General Avion 4900 / Oracle7 (November 1996)
metric_value: 936 users; 4,717 SAPS; 283,000 dialogue steps/hour; WinNT 3.51; 512 MB RAM
notes: Peak Wintel performer despite only 512MB RAM vs HP's 2048MB

metric_name: SAP SD benchmark champion — Sun UltraEnterprise 6000 (Unix)
metric_value: 1,800 users; 20-way UltraSPARC 167MHz SMP; 5,632 MB RAM; Oracle7; 9x8-way application servers
source_page: NT or Unix for R/3 section
notes: 2x users vs NT/Oracle benchmarks; 20% faster response; 2x throughput; 4-6x cost

metric_name: SAP R/3 NT installed base
metric_value: >2,000 SAP systems installed in NT environments as of Q1 1997
source_page: NT or Unix for R/3 section
notes: More than 24% of SAP total installed base

metric_name: Percentage of new R/3 licenses on NT in Q4 1996
metric_value: 40% of all new R/3 licenses shipped in Q4 1996 were NT
source_page: NT or Unix for R/3 section
notes: Dominant choice contradicting conventional mixed NT/Unix wisdom

metric_name: SAP SD benchmark SAPS definition
metric_value: 100 SAPS = 2000 order line items fully processed per hour = 6000 dialogue steps + 2400 SAP transactions

metric_name: Wintel vs Unix price-performance ratio (TPC-C)
metric_value: Wintel with SQL Server: 35-50% of Unix absolute throughput at 1/4 to 1/10 the cost
notes: Wintel price-performance advantage significant despite absolute performance gap

metric_name: SQL Server vs Oracle7 performance gap for R/3 NT
metric_value: SQL Server v6.5 achieves within 20% of Oracle7 performance in SAP SD benchmark
notes: Aberdeen interprets this as SQL Server viable for mid-range NT deployments

metric_name: NT capability trajectory prediction
metric_value: NT will challenge Unix at all performance levels within next 12-18 months (i.e., by late 1998 / early 1999)

metric_name: NT (Windows 2000) vs Unix for SAP ERP outcome
metric_value: Windows 2000 Server became dominant SAP mid-range platform by 2000; NT/Wintel dominated mid-range ERP market as predicted
notes: Prediction confirmed; Windows 2000 released Feb 2000 accelerated Wintel ERP dominance

metric_name: NT for mid-range R/3 readiness assessment
metric_value: NT suitable for organizations with up to 250-500 concurrent users per data server as of Q1 1997
notes: Based on production confirmations from interviewed NT licensees

metric_name: Oracle7 vs SQL Server for R/3 NT
metric_value: Oracle7 current performance leader for R/3-for-NT; SQL Server rapidly narrowing gap
notes: Aberdeen has confirmed SQL Server in production for up to 150 concurrent users

metric_name: SAP Benchmark Council certifications at time of publication
metric_value: 27+ Standard Application Benchmarks certified; 6 Wintel; 30+ total by publication

metric_name: SAP benchmark limitation: OLTP-only
metric_value: SAP SD benchmarks reflect only OLTP; real world mixes OLTP/reporting/DSS
source_page: NT or Unix for R/3 section
notes: Aberdeen caution: extrapolation to real environments requires adjustment

metric_name: SAP benchmark limitation: single application
metric_value: Benchmarks test single R/3 module (SD); real world runs multiple concurrent modules
source_page: NT or Unix for R/3 section

metric_name: SAP benchmark limitation: no site-specific customization
metric_value: Application customization and user behavior create significant real-world variance
source_page: NT or Unix for R/3 section

metric_name: SAP competitive strategy for mid-range
metric_value: Goliath moving down the food chain; premium product — both functionally and price-wise — entering mid-range market

metric_name: J.D. Edwards strategic vulnerability
metric_value: Slow to make no-holds-barred commitment to NT; AS/400 roots create competitive risk
notes: Outcome verified: JDE acquired by PeopleSoft 2003; AS/400 market eroded by NT as predicted

metric_name: Aberdeen overall R/3 on NT verdict
metric_value: Wintel confirmed as viable enterprise platform for mid-range SAP; organizations should evaluate for deployments under 500 users

metric_name: SAP SD benchmark response time standard
metric_value: <2 second average response time standard for all published SAP benchmarks

metric_name: SQL Server mid-range viability prediction
metric_value: Microsoft SQL Server has gained sufficient scalability into mid-range; viable alternative for many medium-sized R/3 NT implementations

metric_name: Microsoft SQL Server market position for SAP
metric_value: SQL Server became leading database for SAP NT deployments by 2000; closed performance gap as predicted
notes: Study noted SQL Server already preferred by new NT licensees; trend continued through 2000s
